'''Greej''' was an elderly [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Near-Human]] who was held prisoner by [[Starhunter's Intergalactic Menagerie]], until Price [[Coby]], [[Jann Tosh]], and his [[Droid/Legends|droids]] set him free. He helped them find [[Ingey]] and defeat the Starhunters, with the later intetion of bringing back all the abducted animals with the help of [[Coby]].

==Biography==

=== Early life and imprisoment ===
Greej, an individual with three-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]],<ref name=":0" /> was once a caretaker<ref name=":1">{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-2|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref> of an animal sanctuary. He wanted to return the animals to their natural habitats, but couldn't due having no money and no partners. Eventually [[Stroon]], the owner of the [[Starhunter's Intergalactic Menagerie]], imprisoned him<ref name=":0" /> to keep from interfering.<ref name=":1" /> He was placed alongside other animals in caves adapted as cages on [[Dandelo]],<ref name=":0">{{DroidsCite|Coby and the Starhunters}}</ref> a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="AOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref>

=== Escape ===
By [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],<ref name="Date">{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}} states that [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] is set 15 years before [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of '' A New Hope'' in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], meaning that ''Star Wars: Droids'' takes place in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]].</ref> the Starhunters had planned to sell him as an "exotic lifeform" to aliens living in distant parts of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. That year he ended up sharing his cell with [[Ingey]], a [[Tessellated Arboreal binjinphant]] abducted on the spaceport of [[Zallakesh]] on [[Chuzalla]]. He was freed by Coby, who had reached the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] to retrieve his pet and helped [[Skirmish on Dandelo|defeat]] the Starhunters. Later the young [[Tammuz-an/Legends|Tammuz-an]] decided to declined enrollment at the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]], choosing instead to help Greej relocate the animals the Starhunters had taken<ref name=":0" /> from their natural habitats.<ref name=":1" />

==Physical appearance==
Greej appeared to be [[Human/Legends|Human]], except he has three-fingered hands.<ref name=":0" /> An old man<ref name=":1" /> by the year [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> he had dark blue eyes and brown hair. He wore a dark brown jump-suit with a tan vest and hat.<ref name=":0" />

== Behind the scenes ==
Greej made its first appearances in "[[Coby and the Starhunters]]", the ninth episode of the TV series [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO'']],<ref name=":0" /> which first aired in [[November 2]], [[1985]].<ref name="Insider27">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref> He was voiced by [[John Stocker]],<ref name=":0" /> who had also voiced several chacters in ''Droids'' and [[Ewoks (television series)|''Star Wars: Ewoks'']].
